person.name: seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Roye

km.JAN 1380: ខែមករា ឆ្នាំ 1380
km.Roye, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France: រ៉ុយ, soមែ, picaរឌិe, ប្រទេសបារាំង

zh.lifeSummary: 當seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Roye於1329年出生時，他的父親Matthieu De Roye III67歲，母親Margaret de Ville34歲。他於1350年與Jeanne de Cherisy結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1345年登記服兵役。他去世於1380年1月享年51歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Roye于1329年出生时，他的父亲Matthieu De Roye III67岁，母亲Margaret de Ville34岁。他于1350年与Jeanne de Cherisy结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1345年登记服兵役。他去世于1380年1月享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Plessier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Gerhardt, Hans.  Dutch Dirk.
German: habitational name for someone from Plesse in Hesse or Plessen in Westphalia.
East German: variant of   Pless   1.

variants.name: Pless
meanings: East German: habitational name from any of various places called with (Old) Slavic pleso ‘swamp’, for example Pless in Upper Silesia, Plessa on the Elster River.
German: variant of   Bless  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Roye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Roy   2.
Americanized form of Croatian Roje: from a pet form of Slavic compound personal names based on the element rod ‘generation, family, relatives’, e.g. Rodimir.
Germanized or Americanized form of Sorbian Roj, Rój or Roja (see Roy 4).

variants.name: Roy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Lucien, Normand, Andre, Jacques, Marcel, Emile, Laurent, Pierre, Cecile, Donat, Gilles.  Indian Dipak, Sumit, Amit, Ajit, Dipankar, Partha, Ranjan, Shyamal, Aloke, Amitava, Chitra, Gautam.
Scottish: nickname for a person with red hair or a ruddy complexion, from Gaelic ruadh ‘red’. Compare Reed .
English (of Norman origin): from Old French roi ‘king’, used as a nickname (see 3 below) and also as a personal name. Compare Ray 2.

nameMeanings.name: Mathieu
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament. See Matthew .

variants.name: Matthew
meanings: form of the name of the Christian evangelist, author of the first gospel in the New Testament. His name is a form of the Hebrew name Mattathia, meaning ‘gift of God’, which is fairly common in the Old Testament, being rendered in the Authorized Version in a number of different forms: Mattan(i)ah, Mattatha(h), Mattithiah, Mattathias, and so on. In the Authorized Version, the evangelist is regularly referred to as Matthew, while the apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot is distinguished as Matthias . A related name from the same Hebrew roots, but reversed, is Jonathan . Throughout the English-speaking world Matthew has been particularly popular since the 1970s.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Lewis   or respelling of   Lou  , as borne by the impresario  Lew Grade  (  1906–98 , originally  Louis Winogradsky ).

nameMeanings.name: Flamand
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Colette, Julien, Ludger.
French: ethnic name for a Fleming, someone from Flanders, from Old French flamenc.

variants.name: Flammang
meanings: Luxembourgish, German, and French (Lorraine): altered form of French Flamand . The surname Flammang is also found in the Luxembourgish- and German- speaking parts of Wallonia (Belgium).

en.lifeSummary: When seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Roye was born in 1329, his father, Matthieu De Roye III, was 67 and his mother, Margaret de Ville, was 34. He married Jeanne de Cherisy in 1350. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He registered for military service in 1345. He died in January 1380, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Roye est né en 1329, son père, Matthieu De Roye III, avait 67 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Ville, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Jeanne de Cherisy en 1350. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1345. Il est décédé en janvier 1380, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Roye 1329 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Matthieu De Roye III 67 und seine Mutter Margaret de Ville 34. Er hat 1350 Jeanne de Cherisy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1345 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb im Januar 1380 im Alter von 51 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Roye è nato il nel 1329, il suo padre, Matthieu De Roye III, aveva 67 anni e il suo madre, Margaret de Ville, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Jeanne de Cherisy nel 1350. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1345. Morì nel gennaio 1380, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.Roye, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France: フランスピカーディソンメロイ
ja.lifeSummary: seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Royeが1329年に生まれた時，父Matthieu De Roye IIIは67歳，母Margaret de Villeは34歳であった。1350年にJeanne de Cherisyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1345年に兵役登録をした。彼は1380年1月に51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Roye(이)가 1329년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Matthieu De Roye III(은)는 67세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret de Ville(은)는 34세였다. 그는 1350년 Jeanne de Cherisy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1345년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1380년 1월 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Roye nasceu em 1329, seu pai, Matthieu De Roye III, tinha 67 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Ville, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Jeanne de Cherisy em 1350. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1345. Ele faleceu em janeiro de 1380, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Roye родился в 1329 г., его отцу по имени Matthieu De Roye III было 67 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Ville, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Jeanne de Cherisy в 1350 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. В 1345 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер январь 1380 г. в возрасте 51 год.

sm.from 1345 to 1349: mai le 1345 e o'o I le 1349

es.lifeSummary: Cuando seigneur Mathieu IV le Flamand du Plessier de Roye nació en 1329, su papá, Matthieu De Roye III, tenía 67 años y su mamá, Margaret de Ville, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Jeanne de Cherisy en 1350. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1345. Murió en enero de 1380, a la edad de 51 años.
